sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Halliburton (NYSE:HAL) Reaches New 1-Year High Following Dividend Announcement
feed.summary: Halliburton (NYSE:HAL) reached a new 52-week high after announcing a dividend of $0.17 per share, payable on March 25th to shareholders of record on March 4th. The company's stock traded higher, reaching $35.61 with a volume of over 1.5 million shares. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" rating for HAL with an average price target of $34.95, and the firm recently beat quarterly earnings estimates.

feed.title: Stibbe advises ElevenEs on Series B first closing backed by Caterpillar Venture Capital
feed.summary: Stibbe advised ElevenEs, a battery technology company, on the first closing of its Series B investment round, which received backing from Caterpillar Venture Capital, Inc. and an affiliate of BST HK Ltd. This investment will support ElevenEs' growth strategy, including the construction of a battery manufacturing facility in Subotica, Serbia. Stibbe provided legal support for the transaction's structuring and implementation.

feed.title: How (HYUP) Movements Inform Risk Allocation Models
feed.summary: The article discusses how Xtrackers High Beta High Yield Bond Etf (NASDAQ: HYUP) movements inform risk allocation models, highlighting a neutral sentiment across all time horizons. It presents institutional trading strategies tailored to different risk profiles, including position trading, momentum breakout, and risk hedging, along with multi-timeframe signal analysis. The analysis suggests a wait-and-see approach with a favorable risk-reward setup.

feed.title: Rtx Corp stock hits all-time high of 206.51 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Rtx Corp (RTX) stock reached an all-time high of $206.51, reflecting strong investor confidence and robust performance with a 66.36% increase over the past year. The company, an aerospace and defense giant, has maintained dividend payments for 56 consecutive years and recently secured significant contracts, including a $230.5 million deal for F135 propulsion systems and technology for sharing radio frequencies. Jefferies maintains a Hold rating with a $225 price target, projecting growth for Pratt & Whitney.

feed.title: Here's What the Street is Saying About Amgen Inc. (AMGN)
feed.summary: Amgen Inc. (AMGN) recently experienced a 'Hold' downgrade from Freedom Capital despite a strong Q4 2025 report, with a revised price target of $375 due to fair valuation and looming exclusivity losses. Concurrently, the European Commission approved UPLIZNA® for generalized myasthenia gravis, offering a new treatment option for patients. The company also reported robust financial performance for fiscal Q4 and full-year 2025, with double-digit growth in revenues and EPS.

feed.title: Xcel Energy Stock After the Wildfire Settlement: Value or Value Trap?
feed.summary: Xcel Energy has recently reached a multi-billion dollar wildfire settlement, leading to a re-evaluation of its stock by investors. While the settlement addresses a major legal overhang, the article explores whether the company represents a value opportunity or a value trap, contingent on regulatory decisions, interest rates, and future climate risks. The analysis emphasizes Xcel's regulated utility model, its energy transition investments, and the critical factors that will determine its stock performance and classification as a stable income play or a risk-discounted story for US investors.

feed.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Is Up 8.7% After Strong Q1 Results And Major AI-Focused Expansion Moves
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) reported strong Q1 2026 results with significant revenue and net income, alongside resolving an export-control matter. The company is heavily investing in AI infrastructure, including new chipmaking tools and a $5 billion R&D center, and projects strong revenue for Q2 2026. These developments reinforce its growth narrative, driven by AI demand and recent customer spending, despite ongoing geopolitical risks related to export controls.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams Labor Lawsuit Tests Employment Practices And Investor Risk Outlook
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams is facing a class action lawsuit in California alleging labor law violations, including unpaid wages and denied breaks. This lawsuit introduces new risks for investors, potentially leading to financial liabilities, reputational damage, and operational changes for the company. Despite these challenges, Sherwin-Williams recently extended its credit facility and maintained its dividend, indicating ongoing financial stability while the legal process unfolds.

feed.title: Assessing Prologis (PLD) Valuation After Toxic Gas Verdict And Growing Legal Liabilities
feed.summary: Prologis (PLD) is facing increased legal risks and liabilities following a toxic gas verdict at one of its warehouses, potentially impacting its valuation. Despite recent positive share price momentum and a dividend increase, the market is assessing whether these factors, along with a "20% Overvalued" narrative among analysts, fully reflect the company's prospects. The article suggests evaluating the underlying data and risks, including potential challenges from slower leasing and higher vacancy levels, to stress-test the current valuation story.

feed.title: Elliott Pressure On Norwegian Cruise Line Raises Questions On Value And Governance
feed.summary: Elliott Investment Management has acquired a significant stake in Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NYSE:NCLH) and is pushing for leadership and board changes. This activist campaign follows recent executive decisions and aims to address concerns regarding the company's governance and performance. Investors are now closely watching how these potential changes could impact the cruise line's future direction, risk profile, and opportunities.

feed.title: A Look At Motorola Solutions (MSI) Valuation After Strong Q4 Earnings And AI Product Momentum
feed.summary: Motorola Solutions saw a significant stock jump after strong Q4 2025 earnings, driven by double-digit revenue growth and AI-enhanced product momentum. Valued at $451.60, with a fair value estimate of $487.90, the company appears undervalued due to its recurring software and services growth, although a high P/E ratio suggests some valuation risk. Investors are encouraged to consider both the growth potential from smart city technologies and risks such as slower software adoption or new market competitors.

feed.title: Omnicell trying to close in on key technical measure
feed.summary: This article states that Omnicell is approaching a key technical measure. Without further content, specific details about this measure or Omnicell's performance cannot be provided. The article suggests an imminent development regarding OMN's stock.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts See Devon Energy (DVN) as a Buy: Should You Invest?
feed.summary: The article discusses whether investors should consider Devon Energy (DVN) based on Wall Street analyst recommendations. While DVN has an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) suggesting a "Strong Buy" to "Buy," the author cautions against relying solely on these ratings due to potential bias. Instead, it suggests validating such recommendations with the proprietary Zacks Rank, which points to a "Sell" for DVN, indicating potential near-term stock price decline.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Raises Price Target for Global Payments (GPN) to $105
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has increased its price target for Global Payments (GPN) to $105 from $103, maintaining an "Overweight" rating. This positive adjustment by analyst Jason Kupferberg indicates confidence in the company's growth potential. Although other analysts have shown mixed sentiment with varying price targets, the average target among 26 analysts sits at $101.36, suggesting a 26.12% upside from the current price.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison earnings on deck after rate case approval By Investing.com
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED) is set to report its fourth-quarter earnings after state regulators approved a three-year rate plan. Analysts anticipate adjusted earnings of $0.85 per share on revenue of $3.71 billion, reflecting a decline in earnings year-over-year but a slight increase in revenue. The utility faces challenges balancing infrastructure spending with affordability concerns, and its ability to maintain its dividend growth streak will be a key focus for investors.

feed.title: PODD Q4 Deep Dive: Type 2 Diabetes Expansion and Innovation Drive Outperformance
feed.summary: Insulet Corporation (NASDAQ: PODD) reported strong Q4 CY2025 results, outperforming revenue expectations with a 31.2% year-on-year sales increase. The company's success is largely attributed to record new customer starts, particularly in type 2 diabetes management and international market expansion, driven by the Omnipod system. Insulet anticipates continued growth through further penetration of automated insulin delivery systems and ongoing product innovation, despite facing tougher year-over-year comparisons.

feed.title: Paycom Software (PAYC) Appoints Shane Hadlock as New President
feed.summary: Paycom Software (PAYC) has appointed Shane Hadlock as its new President and Chief Client Officer, effective February 18, 2026. Hadlock, who has been with Paycom for nearly 14 years and possesses over 30 years of technology experience, will now serve as the company's sole principal operating officer. This strategic leadership change aims to strengthen Paycom's executive team and align with market demands, with Chad Richison continuing as CEO and Chairman.

feed.title: Allegion at Barclays Conference: Strategic Growth and Challenges
feed.summary: Allegion PLC presented its strategic outlook at the Barclays 43rd Annual Industrial Select Conference, emphasizing strong non-residential growth and innovative electronic solutions, while acknowledging challenges in the residential sector. The company plans strategic acquisitions, particularly in electronics, and aims for international market expansion, leveraging connected lock data for new services. Allegion anticipates continued organic growth in non-residential markets and double-digit growth in electronics, with a focus on profitability and margin expansion despite expected softness in the residential market.

feed.title: PayPal Holdings, Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations - Contact The Gross Law Firm Before April 20, 2026 to Discuss Your Rights - PYPL – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: The Gross Law Firm has issued a notice to shareholders of PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL) inviting them to contact the firm regarding potential lead plaintiff appointment in a class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal provided investors with materially false and misleading statements concerning its financial targets and growth potential for its Branded Checkout segment. This announcement follows a significant stock price drop after PayPal revealed disappointing Q4 and full fiscal year 2025 earnings, withdrew 2027 financial targets, and announced a CEO transition.

feed.title: CF Industries stock hits 52-week high at $104.49
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings Inc. (NYSE: CF) stock reached a 52-week high of $104.49, reflecting a 29.79% increase over the past year and a 24.56% year-to-date return. The company maintains a 2.09% dividend yield for 22 consecutive years and possesses strong financial health, despite being considered slightly overvalued. Recent developments include a quarterly dividend announcement, a pilot project for low-carbon fertilizer, and an analyst downgrade due to concerns about normalizing fertilizer prices, offering investors varied perspectives on its future.

feed.title: AbbVie declares quarterly dividend of $1.73 per share
feed.summary: AbbVie Inc. has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.73 per share, payable on May 15, 2026, to stockholders of record as of April 15, 2026. This translates to an annual payout of $6.92 per share, with a current yield of approximately 3.03%. The company, part of the S&P Dividend Aristocrats Index, has increased its dividend by over 330% since its founding in 2013, following strong financial results for Q4 2025 and a credit rating upgrade by Moody's Ratings.

feed.title: Genesis Energy LP stock hits 52-week high at 17.9 USD
feed.summary: Genesis Energy LP's stock reached a 52-week high of $17.9, showing a 50.4% total return over the past year and maintaining dividend payments for 30 consecutive years with a 4.07% yield. Despite this strong performance and investor sentiment, InvestingPro analysis indicates the stock might be overvalued, burdened by over $3 billion in debt, and has not been profitable in the last twelve months. The company recently missed Q4 2025 earnings expectations and is engaged in financial maneuvers, including expanding a cash tender offer and an upsized public offering of senior notes.

feed.title: What Does the Street Have to Say About Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMY)?
feed.summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMY) has partnered with Life Science Cares to combat food insecurity, investing $30 million and aiming for 30,000 volunteer hours by 2030. Despite a reiterated Hold rating from Bernstein and an Underweight rating from Morgan Stanley, the company reported a 1% growth in fiscal Q4 2025 revenue and significant increases in growth portfolio revenue. BMY's strong financial performance and its role as a biopharmaceutical company in various therapeutic areas are highlighted.

feed.title: Mativ Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:MATV) to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.10
feed.summary: Mativ Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:MATV) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.10 per share, payable on March 27th to shareholders of record as of March 13th. This represents an annualized dividend yield of 2.9% and is covered by the company's earnings, with analysts forecasting a future payout ratio of 28.6%. The company recently beat its earnings per share estimates for the last quarter.

feed.title: Adam Goldstein Backs Changes at Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings
feed.summary: Adam Goldstein, former Royal Caribbean International president and CEO, has confirmed his collaboration with Elliott Investment Management to advocate for changes to Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings' board. In a Fortune opinion piece, Goldstein expressed his belief that Norwegian possesses industry-leading assets but has lagged financially compared to its peers. He emphasized that if appointed to the board, he would serve all shareholders and stakeholders, focusing on disciplined execution to restore Norwegian to a leadership position.

feed.title: “A Super Simple Short Thesis” Targets Super Group (SGHC) Limited (SGHC) Shares
feed.summary: Spruce Point Capital Management published a "Super Simple Short Thesis" report, forecasting a potential long-term downside of 20% to 50% for Super Group (SGHC) Limited shares. This contrasts with Benchmark's previous Buy rating and $17 price target, driven by strong casino performance and a positive FY2025 outlook. Super Group, an online sports betting and gaming operator, also announced a special cash dividend, citing a solid balance sheet and strong cash generation.

feed.title: Australia Data Centre Construction Industry Report 2025 Featuring AirTrunk , Multiplex Constructions, Fujitsu, Procore Technologies, Kapitol, Nilsen, Kajima, and DXN - Growth Analysis Forecast to 2034
feed.summary: The "Australia Data Centre Construction Market Size, Share, Growth Analysis Report and Forecast Trends (2025-2034)" report projects the Australian data centre construction market to grow from AUD 3.64 billion in 2025 to AUD 6.24 billion by 2034, at a CAGR of 5.54%. This growth is driven by advancements in mechanical and electrical systems, a focus on sustainable design, and increasing demand from sectors like BFSI due to stringent data security needs. Key players in this market include AirTrunk, Multiplex Constructions, Fujitsu, and Procore Technologies.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of Inovio Pharmaceuticals, Inc. Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Inovio Pharmaceuticals, Inc. on behalf of investors who purchased securities between October 10, 2023, and December 26, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Inovio made false and misleading statements regarding deficiencies in manufacturing for its CELLECTRA device and the submission timeframe and eligibility for FDA accelerated approval of its INO-3107 Biologics License Application. Investors suffered damages following the FDA's announcement on December 29, 2025, that it accepted the BLA on a standard review timeline, stating Inovio did not submit adequate information for accelerated approval.

feed.title: DanceOne and Invisalign Partner to Bring Confidence to Dancers in Chicago, Fort Lauderdale and Atlanta
feed.summary: DanceOne, a leading producer of dance events, has partnered with Align Technology's Invisalign to bring confidence-boosting experiences to young dancers. The partnership will feature interactive activations at upcoming competitions in Chicago, Fort Lauderdale, and Atlanta, offering AI Digital Smart Mirrors, parent lounges, and oral health scans. Additionally, DanceOne and Invisalign will provide scholarships for dancers to attend future events, including the prestigious DancerPalooza.

feed.title: GE HealthCare wins FDA nod for next-gen Signa MRI tech
feed.summary: GE HealthCare has secured FDA clearance for three new MRI offerings: Signa Sprint with Freelium, Signa Bolt, and Signa One. These innovations aim to address radiology challenges by providing smarter, faster, and more sustainable imaging solutions. The new technologies include helium-free MRI, advanced 3T systems, and an AI-driven ecosystem for workflow efficiency and improved patient care.

feed.title: Pool Corp Issues 2025 Results and 2026 EPS Outlook
feed.summary: Pool Corporation (POOL) reported stable 2025 net sales of $5.3 billion, but net income and diluted EPS decreased due to higher operating expenses despite improved gross margin. The company increased inventory and debt to support operations and share repurchases, and it provided 2026 EPS guidance of $10.85 to $11.15, signaling modest sales growth expectations. Analyst ratings and TipRanks' AI Analyst suggest a "Hold" or "Neutral" stance, citing financial stability and revenue growth tempered by margin compression and a long-term downtrend.

feed.title: Paycom Elevates Shane Hadlock to President and CCO
feed.summary: Paycom has promoted Shane Hadlock to President and Chief Client Officer, making him the sole principal operating officer and increasing his base salary to $700,000. This move reflects Paycom's focus on client-centric operations and its automation strategy, with Hadlock overseeing client-focused and operational aspects. The company's board also amended bylaws to redefine the president's role and broaden the definition of principal competitors, enhancing governance flexibility.

feed.title: Advisor Pulse – Winter 2025
feed.summary: Blackstone's "Advisor Pulse – Winter 2025" report highlights financial advisors' views on diversification strategies amid elevated equity valuations and public market volatility. Advisors see private markets, particularly private real assets, as key for clients seeking long-term capital appreciation, enhanced diversification, and potential inflation mitigation. The survey indicates a strong anticipation for increased allocations to private markets this year.

feed.title: AbbVie (ABBV) Maintains Quarterly Dividend of $1.73/Share
feed.summary: AbbVie Inc. (ABBV) will maintain its quarterly dividend at $1.73 per share, providing a forward yield of 3.04%. The pharmaceutical giant, with a market capitalization of $400.55 billion, continues to be a dominant player in healthcare, despite some financial health concerns such as declining margins and high leverage. The company's valuation metrics suggest it might be overvalued, though institutional ownership remains strong.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Northrop Grumman, Danaos and Tronox Holdings
feed.summary: Northrop Grumman Corp (NOC), Danaos Corp (DAC), and Tronox Holdings PLC (TROX) are set to trade ex-dividend on February 23, 2026. This article details their upcoming quarterly dividend payments, their respective dividend yields, and provides historical dividend charts for each company. It also advises investors to consider dividend history for stability when estimating future annual yields.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Boise Cascade, Peabody Energy and International Paper
feed.summary: Boise Cascade Co. (BCC), Peabody Energy Corp (BTU), and International Paper Co (IP) are set to trade ex-dividend on February 23, 2026. Boise Cascade will pay a quarterly dividend of $0.22, Peabody Energy $0.075, and International Paper $0.4625. Based on recent stock prices, this translates to approximate dividend yields of 0.26% for BCC, 0.23% for BTU, and 0.95% for IP, which investors should account for on the ex-dividend date.

feed.title: PPL Scheduled to Announce Q4 Earnings on February 20
feed.summary: PPL Corporation is set to release its Q4 earnings on February 20, with a consensus EPS estimate of $0.42 and projected revenue of $2.42 billion, indicating significant year-over-year growth. The company has a strong historical record of beating EPS and revenue estimates, though recent revisions show a cautious market outlook. Analyst ratings are generally positive, with Mizuho and BMO Capital raising their price targets and maintaining "Neutral" and "Outperform" ratings, respectively.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Bank Of NT Butterfield & Son, Infineon Technologies and Patrick Industries
feed.summary: Bank Of NT Butterfield & Son (NTB), Infineon Technologies (IFNNF), and Patrick Industries (PATK) are set to trade ex-dividend on February 23, 2026. This article details their upcoming dividend payouts, ex-dividend dates, and the approximate percentage by which their stock prices are expected to drop, all else being equal, when they trade ex-dividend. It also provides historical dividend charts and estimated annualized yields for each company.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Wins 3 FDA Nods for Next-Gen SIGNA MRI Tech
feed.summary: GE HealthCare has received three FDA 510(k) clearances for new magnetic resonance (MR) technologies: SIGNA Sprint with Freelium (a 1.5T sealed magnet MRI system), SIGNA Bolt (a 3T MRI scanner), and SIGNA One (an AI-driven workflow ecosystem). These innovations aim to address rising procedural demand, increase efficiency, and sustainability in radiology by providing smarter, faster, and more consistent imaging solutions. The company said these advancements will help meet the growing challenges faced by healthcare providers, including an aging population, workforce burnout, and increasing disease incidences.

feed.title: Opendoor earnings up next: Can it prove unit economics work? By Investing.com
feed.summary: Opendoor Technologies Inc. is set to release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results, with analysts expecting a loss of 12 cents per share on revenue of $576.9 million, a significant decline from the previous quarter. Investors will be closely watching for signs that the iBuying company can achieve sustainable profitability and demonstrate consistent unit economics, especially given the challenging housing market. The company's cash management, balance sheet health, and detailed 2026 outlook will be key areas of focus.

feed.title: QUANTA SERVICES, INC. SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Quanta Services, Inc. released its 2025 Form 10-K report detailing significant financial growth with revenues of $28.48 billion and net income of $1.04 billion, driven by increased demand in Electric and Underground and Infrastructure segments. The company expanded through strategic acquisitions, including Dynamic Systems, and anticipates continued strong demand from electrification trends and infrastructure upgrades, while also acknowledging operational, financial, and macroeconomic risks inherent in its industry.

feed.title: CF Industries Stock Surges on Clean Energy Reporting Amid Price Target Jan’26 Increase
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings Inc. (NYSE: CF) stock surged by 9.54% following its strong Q4 2025 performance, beating EPS and net sales expectations. The company's commitment to sustainable agriculture, including plans for a low-carbon fertilizer chain and significant capital returns to shareholders, has bolstered investor confidence. RBC also uplifted its price target for CF Industries from $90 to $95, further highlighting its positive outlook.

feed.title: Omnicom Group’s Strategic Moves Signal Growth Amid Market Volatility
feed.summary: Omnicom Group's stock saw a 10.89% increase driven by a JPMorgan price target raise to $117 and a substantial $5 billion share repurchase program. Despite reporting increased Q4 EPS, the company missed revenue expectations due to challenges from strategic acquisitions like IPG. Omnicom plans to double cost synergy targets and enhance its data/tech platform to drive future growth and operational efficiency amidst market volatility.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum Surges with Strong Q4 Earnings Beat
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum (OXY) reported robust Q4 earnings, with adjusted EPS of $0.77 significantly surpassing market consensus, driven by operational efficiency and cost management. The company's strategic sale of OxyChem for $5.8 billion further strengthened its balance sheet and cash flow, enabling debt reduction and investment in future ventures. This strong performance and strategic positioning have led to increased investor confidence and upgraded price targets from analysts, despite competitive pressures in the global oil market.

feed.title: What Do Analysts Have to Say About Gilead Sciences (GILD) Post Earnings?
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences (GILD) reported strong fiscal Q4 and full-year 2025 results, driven by growth in Biktarvy and Descovy, and the launch of Yeztugo. Following these positive results, several analysts, including Needham, Scotiabank, and BofA, raised their price targets and maintained bullish ratings on the stock. Analysts are confident in GILD's 2026 growth, with BofA no longer modeling cannibalization of Descovy sales by Yeztugo.

feed.title: Here’s What the Street is Saying About Amgen Inc. (AMGN)
feed.summary: Amgen Inc. (NASDAQ: AMGN) was recently downgraded to Hold from Buy by Freedom Capital, which adjusted its price target to $375, citing that the shares are at fair value despite a strong Q4 report. Concurrently, Amgen announced European Commission approval for UPLIZNA® for generalized myasthenia gravis and reported significant revenue and EPS growth for fiscal Q4 and full-year 2025. While Amgen is a biotechnology company with potential, the article suggests that certain AI stocks may offer more promising returns.

feed.title: Board Member Of Royal Caribbean Group Sold $91.17M In Stock
feed.summary: Arne Alexander Wilhelmsen, a Board Member at Royal Caribbean Group (NYSE: RCL), sold 280,000 shares totaling $91.17 million on February 18. This transaction comes as Royal Caribbean Group faces a negative revenue trend, showing a 17.1% decline in revenue growth by December 31, 2025. Despite financial challenges, the company maintains a strong market capitalization and a sound debt-to-equity ratio, although its EPS and gross margin are below industry averages.

feed.title: Adobe opens Photoshop, Acrobat and Firefly AI free to Indian students
feed.summary: Adobe has launched an initiative to provide Indian students enrolled in accredited higher education institutions with free access to Photoshop, Acrobat, and its generative AI platform, Firefly. This program, in partnership with the Indian government, aims to enhance digital and AI skills among students by integrating professional-grade tools into their formal education. The company seeks to foster early familiarity with its platforms, strengthen its market position, and align with India's efforts to boost employability in an AI-driven economy.

feed.title: Paycom promotes Shane Hadlock to president, effective immediately
feed.summary: Paycom Software, Inc. has promoted Shane Hadlock to President and Chief Client Officer, effective immediately. Hadlock will oversee strategic operations and the company's automation strategy, bringing nearly 14 years of experience at Paycom and previous leadership roles at Hertz. This announcement follows recent news of Paycom's 2026 revenue guidance falling short of market expectations, leading to adjusted price targets from several analyst firms.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: N-able Q4 2025 reports 12% revenue growth
feed.summary: N-able Inc. reported its Q4 2025 financial results, showing a 12% year-over-year revenue growth to $130 million, beating expectations, but its EPS of $0.06 missed the $0.10 forecast. Despite the EPS miss, the company's stock surged 8.83% pre-market, driven by strong revenue performance, 12% ARR growth, and strategic AI initiatives like the new workflow assistant, Enzo. N-able projects 8-9% revenue growth for 2026 and plans to continue investing in AI and product innovation to widen its competitive moat.
